conversations_memory:
**Work context**

Johnny Berry Jr. operates multiple active business ventures: Source AI, a web design and AI services business targeting local small businesses (built on LandingSite with the domain thesourceai.io); a custom suit sales practice doing mobile fittings (visiting clients at their homes or offices); and a sales role at Daily Resolve, helping business owners negotiate down MCA and business loan payments. He is also building a personal brand under the name Johnny Berry Jr. on Instagram and TikTok.

**Personal context**

Johnny is based in North Miami, Florida (zip code 33161) and is deeply embedded in Miami's social and nightlife scene. He has an interest in fitness, physique, and wellness, and thinks in terms of systems and reusability across his work and personal projects. Friends Howie and Zach (who works at Tweets Custom Suits) appear in his personal and professional orbit.

**Top of mind**

Johnny recently worked with the Cowork agentic desktop app to build a productivity and income system centered on Source AI, including a client acquisition workflow, reusable site-build scaffolding, and sales assets. He is actively developing his personal brand on Instagram and TikTok, with a focus on talking-head/selfie-style Reels and resolving a conversion gap (strong engagement but low follower growth). He is also refining his sales outreach systems for both the custom suit business and Daily Resolve, building reusable text message templates for prospect re-engagement and lead handling.

**Brief history**

*Recent months*

Johnny has been building out Source AI as his primary business, covering everything from domain and nameserver setup to client delivery workflows and Instagram branding. He completed the technical DNS configuration (pointing thesourceai.io to LandingSite's nameservers) and set up the Source AI Instagram as a business profile. He built a landing page for a friend's personal brand called FORGE (dark/gold aesthetic, Stripe payment hooks, four coaching tiers including a high-ticket "Brotherhood" membership). He also built an Apex Supply Co. dropshipping page and assisted a client (Atlanta Cleaners) with a cinematic luxury single-page site. He has hands-on familiarity with Netlify deployment and DNS management for client work. For Daily Resolve, he developed a working library of text-based sales responses covering the full lead lifecycle, and learned the importance of steering prospects toward a phone call before sending contracts. His suit business outreach relies on warm, low-pressure messaging anchored to concrete hooks (new fabric inventory, referrals through a colleague named Danni). Johnny ran a Shopify dropshipping operation (TurboDrain and a pet hair roller) with active Meta ad campaigns, including creative production via Creatify and campaign optimization in Meta Ads Manager. He has explored the Cowork agentic desktop app as a throughput accelerator for knowledge work.

Johnny's signature design aesthetic — cinematic, dark-themed with gold accents and scroll animations — has been established as a repeatable template context across client builds.

*Earlier context*

Johnny explored nightclub support roles (barback, busser) at Miami venues like E11EVEN, LIV, and Story as supplemental income, with barback identified as the strongest entry path. He evaluated a jet ski rental business opportunity with a detailed financial breakdown for the Miami market. He considered modeling in Miami (commercial, fitness, and promotional tracks) and looked into Miami Swim Week casting. He navigated several logistical challenges including vehicle issues (a catalytic converter problem on an Audi), insurance and license reinstatement questions, and public transit routing in Miami-Dade. He successfully reopened a Florida Reemployment Assistance (unemployment) claim and updated address information in the CONNECT system.

*Long-term background*

Johnny has a long-standing interest in personal development, gamification of habits, and self-optimization (evidenced by a detailed Dark Souls–inspired Life RPG tracker he built). He has explored multiple income streams over time including DoorDash, real estate, CDL trucking, bartending, and remote sales. His communication style is consistently conversational, direct, and systems-oriented — he prefers reusable frameworks and templates over one-off solutions, and values honest, data-grounded advice over vague or overpromising plans.

project_memories.019e339d-53c3-7620-9469-b17c925756ad:
**Purpose & context**

Royalty is the solo founder of Source AI (thesourceai.io), a premium AI-powered web design agency targeting local businesses, based in Miami, FL. Contact: royalty@thesourceai.io / (331) 256-0819 / Google Meet: meet.google.com/rzz-bdmc-qph.

The agency delivers custom, fast, client-owned websites using AI tools (primarily Claude + LandingSite), with Cloudflare for hosting/DNS and Stripe for payments. Royalty operates as a one-person shop with Claude as the primary builder. The core value proposition — AI-powered speed and pricing — is always disclosed upfront and framed as a competitive advantage.

**Primary niches:** Independent restaurants, cafés, and brunch spots (current focus). Secondary targets: med spas/aestheticians or trades (plumbers, roofers, HVAC). Avoid chains, franchises, lawyers, photographers, and oversaturated areas (Wynwood, Brickell). Best prospecting cities: St. Petersburg FL (Grand Central), Jacksonville (Riverside), Tampa Seminole Heights.

---

**Current state**

**Pricing (LOCKED as of May 2026):**

| Tier | Pages | Price |
|------|-------|-------|
| Launch | 1 | $500 (one-time) |
| Growth | 3 | $899 (one-time) |
| Premium | 5 | $1,500 (one-time) |

Optional Care Plan: $45/month or $480/year (saves $60). Pricing is **never** mentioned in cold outreach, flyers, or Instagram bio — revealed verbally on Google Meet only, after demo creates wow-factor. Care Plan pitched post-close as the default ("most clients take it") with easy opt-out.

**Sales motion (manual only — ZYNTHOS automation paused):**
Call/DM/Email → Book Google Meet → Screen-share live LandingSite demo (built before outreach) → Verbal price reveal → Close on Meet → Stripe Payment Link sent within 5 minutes.

**Active leads (as of May 2026):**
1. **Kenyatta** — Warm referral via friend Jason; owns "Luxe Styling" (menswear/styling). Highest priority given warm referral close rates. Discovery email and LandingSite demo in progress.
2. **Coffee Break House** — Miami brunch spot, 7251 Biscayne Blvd; owner email coffeebreak1166@gmail.com. Email sent, awaiting reply.
3. **Miami Kava** — Owners David and Paula, (305) 456-0018. Not yet contacted.
4. **Hilite Coffee** — Jacksonville, no website. Instagram DM planned.

**Completed infrastructure:**
- Source AI site (thesourceai.io) fully live on Cloudflare Workers with cinematic dark aesthetic, particle-network hero, 3D tilt cards, scroll reveals, cursor spotlight, AI lead-qualification chatbot ("Source Assistant"), validated contact form, FAQ with schema, Calendly integration.
- DNS fully migrated from LandingSite/Squarespace nameservers to Cloudflare (lynn/mimi ns). Email DNS verified including manually added DKIM record.
- Luxe Styling LLC site built (for Kenyatta/Aaron); pending deployment to Cloudflare Workers preview URL for client review.
- Stripe live account active. Five Payment Links needed: one per website tier + Care Plan monthly + annual. Stripe branding: #8B5CF6 violet, #F0EBE0 cream, Source AI "S" icon.

---

**On the horizon**

- Deploy Luxe Styling LLC preview to Cloudflare Workers URL and send to Aaron/Kenyatta for review.
- Create all five Stripe Payment Links with proper Source AI branding.
- Execute outreach to Miami Kava (David & Paula) and Hilite Coffee (Instagram DM).
- Follow up on Coffee Break House email.
- Continue prospecting in St. Pete, Jacksonville, and Tampa Seminole Heights.

---

**Key learnings & principles**

- **Close before building complexity.** Royalty has a tendency to add new pricing tiers, tools, cities, or service lines before the current playbook is validated — redirect to closing active warm leads first.
- **Warm referrals are highest priority** (20–40% close rate vs. ~1–3% cold). Kenyatta should always be front of queue.
- **Demo-first discipline:** Demos must be built and ready before outreach begins — never show up to a Meet without one.
- **Domain policy is firm:** Clients always own and register their own domains (Cloudflare or Porkbun recommended; GoDaddy avoided). Royalty never holds credentials or logs in for customers.
- **Cloudflare Worker deployment gotchas learned:** Stale A/CNAME records block Worker hostname assignment and must be deleted first. Root domain custom domain field must be left blank (not typed); subdomain field only gets "www" for the www version — typing the full domain causes doubled entries (e.g., "thesourceai.io.thesourceai.io").
- **AI disclosure is non-negotiable** and framed as a feature explaining speed and competitive pricing.

---

**Approach & patterns**

- Prefers structured tables, clear hierarchy, and concrete numbers — avoid hype or vague framing.
- Direct, honest communicator with strong financial instincts; questions costs and pauses before approvals.
- Has prior sales experience but is newer to web agency sales specifically.
- Iterative builder: provides detailed visual/UX feedback across multiple rounds; responses well to precise implementation over broad suggestions.
- Treats Claude as a collaborative builder, not just an advisor — expects working code and deployable deliverables.

---

**Tools & resources**

- **Builder:** Claude (primary), LandingSite.ai (client demos + project transfer post-payment)
- **Hosting/DNS:** Cloudflare Workers (drag-and-drop deploy), Cloudflare DNS management
- **Payments:** Stripe (live account)
- **Scheduling:** Google Meet (meet.google.com/rzz-bdmc-qph), Calendly (embedded in client sites)
- **Social:** Instagram @thesourceai.io (Business account; goals: Messages + Leads; enabled: Comments, Messages, Leads, Calls, Emails — **not** Texts, as phone is not SMS-monitored)
- **Domain registrars:** Cloudflare or Porkbun (recommended to clients); GoDaddy avoided
- **Brand:** Violet #8B5CF6 / Near-black #0A0A0A / Cream #F0EBE0; Playfair Display italic + Syne for logo type

**Instagram bio (locked, no emojis):**
*"Premium AI-powered websites for local businesses. Custom, fast, and yours forever. Miami-based. Founding clients welcome."*
Link in bio: thesourceai.io
project_memories.019e473f-15a7-7607-a9b3-1ef3073e4112:
**Purpose & context**

Johnny Berry is building a faith-first masculinity, style, and discipline personal brand on Instagram under the handle @johnnyberryjr. The brand blends faith, style, and discipline content aimed at men. Johnny also runs **Source AI** (thesourceai.io), an AI-powered website business targeting local businesses, with a separate Instagram presence. The two brands are kept distinct.

Success looks like growing algorithmic reach and engagement across Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts through high-retention Reels and consistent topical identity. A friend named Jason has been involved in video editing (using CapCut).

---

**Current state**

Johnny recently launched a "22 Habits of Men Who Become Undeniable" Reel — his strongest-performing Reel to date — across Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts. Key takeaway from that launch: **sends were the standout engagement metric**, while **watch time (averaging ~15 seconds on a 74-second video) is the primary constraint on further algorithmic reach** and the top priority to improve in future content.

Active platform considerations:
- **Instagram**: Bio optimized, grid cleaned up, per-post captions rewritten. Analytics reviewed.
- **TikTok**: Bio written within the 80-character limit with faith-first positioning. Johnny was evaluating whether to switch to a free TikTok Business account to enable a clickable website link (thesourceai.io), weighing that against potential reach tradeoffs.
- **YouTube Shorts**: Guidance received on title structure (including #Shorts), description formatting, custom thumbnails, and building a topical series format.

A generated deliverable — the 22 Habits Reel script — exists at `/mnt/user-data/outputs/Reel_Script_22_Habits.docx`.

---

**On the horizon**

- Resolving TikTok Business account decision (link access vs. reach tradeoff)
- Improving Reel hook strength and pacing to increase average watch time
- Building out repeatable Reel series formats for YouTube Shorts channel branding
- Continued cross-platform content distribution strategy

---

**Key learnings & principles**

- **Sends > likes** as an algorithmic signal on Instagram; prioritizing content that people forward
- **Watch time is the primary distribution lever** across all short-form platforms (Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts); retention and swipe-away rate determine reach
- **Transfer-chain recompression** was a recurring video quality issue — texting or DM-ing video files recompresses them each pass; fix is AirDrop or cloud storage (Google Drive/Dropbox) to preserve original export quality
- Instagram recompresses all uploads to 1080p on playback, so exporting at 1080p/30fps at highest bitrate is preferable to 4K (no visible feed benefit, unnecessarily large files)
- Posting and exporting directly from Johnny's own device (rather than routing through Jason's transfer chain) avoids quality degradation
- The #Shorts hashtag in title or description is required for proper YouTube categorization
- On TikTok, emoji-heavy comment floods carry minimal algorithmic value compared to substantive comments

---

**Approach & patterns**

- **Direct and results-oriented**: Johnny prefers clear, actionable recommendations over options-heavy responses and will push back firmly if responses feel evasive
- Cross-platform launches are standard — new content goes to Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube simultaneously
- Prior sessions have involved deep hands-on work (caption rewrites, script generation, video processing, analytics interpretation), so Claude should be prepared to work at that level of detail
- A separate Project chat titled "video content analyzer" exists within the same Claude project (URL: `https://claude.ai/chat/3a2f0e2d-9baf-4830-8a79-f2e01d7d29a8`) and has been used for technical video quality analysis

---

**Tools & resources**

- **CapCut Pro** — video editing (used by Jason)
- **ffmpeg** — used for video processing, audio cleanup, color grading, denoising
- **Claude Project** — ongoing brand-building work, with a separate "video content analyzer" chat within the same project
- **thesourceai.io** — Source AI business website (separate from personal brand)
- **Platforms**: Instagram (@johnnyberryjr), TikTok, YouTube Shorts
project_memories.019e5b76-1bb8-742b-9d00-a2e98c660119:
**Purpose & context**

Johnny Berry is building toward a multi-engine wealth and lifestyle strategy centered on four interconnected pillars: a high-commission sales career, a personal brand in the male self-improvement and menswear space, involvement with a bespoke menswear business called Luxe Styling, and long-term real estate wealth accumulation.

**Luxe Styling** is a custom tailoring business founded by Aaron Tadian, focused on premium fabrics from mills including Holland & Sherry, Scabal, Zegna, Dormeuil, and Loro Piana. It operates via home and office visits by appointment and has press mentions in New York Weekly, CEO Weekly, and US Insider. The brand aesthetic is Mediterranean, linen-heavy, and old-money European.

Johnny's personal brand positioning is "the Modern Gentleman," targeting the male self-improvement and menswear niches on social media. He describes himself as having strong sales and closing skills and is actively pursuing a sales role with uncapped commission potential.

**Current state**

- Pursuing a sales seat with uncapped commission, with a target of reaching a high monthly income level within approximately one year
- Working on or affiliated with Luxe Styling operations and brand development
- In the process of rebuilding the Luxe Styling website; Claude drafted a detailed brand-specific prompt for use with LandingSite.ai, grounded in the brand's visual identity and credentials
- Building toward credit strengthening and lifestyle discipline as foundational steps in the wealth plan

**On the horizon**

- Scaling a personal brand across social media platforms in the menswear and self-improvement niches
- Short-term rental, small multifamily, or boutique real estate acquisition as initial income-producing assets, with a longer-term goal of hotel ownership through equity accumulation
- A structured three-year business plan (produced by Claude as a markdown document) organized into 6-month, 1-year, and 3-year phases, covering: credit building, sales strategy, content platform development, Luxe Styling scaling, and real estate sequencing

**Key learnings & principles**

- Luxury asset ownership (supercars, yachts, high-end watches) is viable at high income levels but requires honest accounting of total cost of ownership, not just purchase price — chartering or renting before committing to ownership is a recommended first step
- Hotel ownership as an investment is not accessible through income alone at early wealth stages; the realistic path runs through equity accumulation in smaller income-producing real estate first
- The four-engine model frames the strategy: sales seat (cash engine) → personal brand (leverage engine) → Luxe Styling (product engine) → real estate (wealth engine)
- For pre-owned luxury vehicles, certified pre-owned status is strongly preferred; common failure points and ownership risks should be evaluated before committing

**Approach & patterns**

- Johnny thinks in structured, phased frameworks and prefers comprehensive, copy-pasteable deliverables (e.g., the full three-year business plan as a single markdown document)
- Engages with hypothetical financial scenarios as a way to stress-test future lifestyle and investment decisions before committing
- Comfortable operating across multiple parallel tracks (sales, brand, business, real estate) simultaneously
- Prefers honest, grounded assessments over optimistic projections — Claude's direct breakdowns of risks and costs were well-received

**Tools & resources**

- **LandingSite.ai** — being used to rebuild the Luxe Styling website
- **Instagram and existing web presence** — used as visual reference for Luxe Styling brand direction
- AI upscaling tools — relevant for print quality work
- Miami-Dade Public Library and UPS Store — referenced for printing needs
project_memories.019e4b6b-65dd-7491-805d-8e8b363238e4:
**Purpose & context**

Johnny Berry Jr. is based in Miami, FL and is actively navigating a career transition or job search across multiple industries. His professional background spans sales, luxury retail (Tweeds Custom Suits — luxury suit consultation), property management (overseeing a large portfolio across multiple communities), housing/social services (Saint Vincent de Paul), and hospitality/fine dining (server and busser roles). He also holds a NSPIRE certification and served in the USMC Infantry. He has expressed interest in roles like Louis Vuitton Client Advisor, where his luxury retail background is seen as a strong differentiator.

**Current state**

Johnny is actively job searching and building out his professional presence. He has multiple tailored resumes in hand — a comprehensive professional version and a hospitality/fine dining-specific version — both in .docx and .pdf formats. He is setting up or optimizing his LinkedIn profile, including correcting an outdated Illinois location to Miami, FL and troubleshooting an email verification issue (likely filtered to Gmail spam/Promotions). He is also supporting a friend (Angielys Rivera Del Valle) with resume creation.

**On the horizon**

- Continuing LinkedIn profile build-out (skills, headline, summary already drafted — ongoing setup in progress)
- Potentially pursuing luxury retail roles (e.g., Louis Vuitton Client Advisor) in the Miami market
- Possible further resume tailoring for additional target roles

**Approach & patterns**

- Johnny works with both a short and long version of source material, suggesting he thinks in terms of adaptable, audience-specific documents
- Prefers print-optimized, polished final deliverables (ink-safe colors, standard margins, both .docx and .pdf)
- Engages in real-time, step-by-step guidance (e.g., LinkedIn setup via screenshots) — appreciates hands-on, iterative support
- Involves himself in helping people in his network (resume assistance for a friend)

**Tools & resources**

- LinkedIn (active setup/optimization in progress)
- Microsoft Word / PDF format for documents
- Gmail (primary email; relevant for LinkedIn verification troubleshooting)
